behavior: A gregarious, noisy ground forager that walks or struts while pecking for food. Often forms large mixed flocks, roosts high in trees or on exposed perches, and flies directly with stiff wingbeats.
field_marks: Large, lanky blackbird with long legs
Long, heavy bill with a slight downward curve
Glossy blue-purple head with iridescent body sheen
Long, graduated tail often held in a shallow V
Long tail makes the wings appear comparatively short
habitat: Open and semi-open habitats with scattered trees, including woodland edges, meadows, marshes, agricultural fields, parks, and suburban lawns; generally avoids extensive unbroken forest.
